paths.title: Run your first familiar in Cave
intents: i want a familiar on my machine
paths.summary: Install the Coven CLI, start the daemon, and create a familiar you can talk to inside Cave.

steps.title: Install the Coven CLI
steps.body: Install the CLI that Cave drives under the hood.

steps.title: Start the Coven daemon
steps.body: Cave talks to a local daemon for sessions and familiars.

steps.title: Create your first familiar
steps.body: Add a named familiar from the Cave sidebar dock, then select it.

steps.title: Say hello
steps.body: Open a chat with your familiar and ask it to do something small to confirm it responds.

paths.successMoment: You can talk to a named familiar and see its work in Cave.

blockers.label: Daemon won't start
blockers.suggestion: Run coven doctor to diagnose the local runtime.

blockers.label: No familiars listed
blockers.suggestion: Add one from the sidebar familiar dock's + button.

paths.title: Set up a coding workspace with CastCodes
intents: i want a desktop ai coding workspace
paths.summary: Use CastCodes for a desktop, project-scoped AI coding workspace where you can run an agent and review its changes.

steps.title: Open CastCodes
steps.body: Launch CastCodes and point it at a project directory.

steps.title: Scope a project
steps.body: Select the repository you want the agent to work in so sessions stay project-scoped.

steps.title: Run an agent session
steps.body: Start a session, give it a task, and watch it work against your project.

steps.title: Review changes
steps.body: Inspect the diff before accepting — CastCodes keeps the agent's work visible and reviewable.

paths.successMoment: You can run a visible, project-scoped agent session and review its changes.

blockers.label: No provider connected
blockers.suggestion: Connect a model provider in CastCodes settings before running a session.

paths.title: Work in the terminal with Coven Code
intents: work in a project from the terminal
paths.summary: Use Coven Code for a terminal/TUI agent: launch it, connect a provider, and work inside a project from the command line.

steps.title: Launch Coven Code
steps.body: Start the terminal agent in your project directory.

steps.title: Connect a provider
steps.body: Configure a model provider so the agent can run.

steps.title: Work in your project
steps.body: Give the agent a task and iterate from the terminal.

paths.successMoment: You can launch Coven Code, connect a provider, and work in a project.

blockers.label: coven: command not found
blockers.suggestion: Install the Coven CLI with npm install -g @opencoven/cli.

paths.title: Understand and build the Coven runtime
intents: i want to understand the runtime
paths.summary: Read the OpenCoven/coven repo to understand Coven as the local harness substrate, then run a daemon-backed session.

steps.title: Read the architecture
steps.body: Skim the README and daemon docs to understand how the harness runs familiars locally.

steps.title: Run a daemon-backed session
steps.body: Start the daemon and run a session to see the substrate in action.

paths.successMoment: You understand Coven as the local harness substrate and can run a daemon-backed session.

blockers.label: Build fails
blockers.suggestion: Run coven doctor and check the runtime prerequisites.

paths.title: Define a familiar with familiar-contract
intents: i want to define a familiar properly
paths.summary: Use the familiar-contract spec to capture a familiar's identity, purpose, memory, authority, and human belonging in a compliant directory.

steps.title: Read the contract
steps.body: Review the familiar-contract spec to learn the required fields.

steps.title: Draft the identity
steps.body: Capture identity, purpose, memory, authority, and human belonging for your familiar.

steps.title: Validate the directory
steps.body: Lay out a compliant familiar directory and validate it against the spec.

paths.successMoment: You have identity, purpose, memory, authority, and human belonging captured in a compliant familiar directory.

blockers.label: Unsure which fields are required
blockers.suggestion: The contract README lists every required field with examples.
